description_long: This is a vintage gramophone-style record player featuring a large, flared metal horn for sound amplification, mounted on a wooden base with a circular platform. The base is box-shaped with ornate metallic accents and a visible hand crank on the side, suggesting manual operation. A black vinyl record with a red label sits atop a round turntable platter, which is centrally positioned on the wooden base. The tonearm, made of metal, is curved and rests near the edge of the record, with intricate detailing at its joints. The overall finish is aged, with visible wear and patina on both the wood and metal components, giving it an antique appearance. The object exudes a sense of historical charm and craftsmanship, making it a striking decorative or functional piece in an urban vintage setting.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large metal horn, wooden base, and visible hand crank, featuring ornate details and an aged finish.
description_view_0: The record player is seen from a front-left angle, highlighting the large flared horn, the vinyl record on the platter, and the ornate wooden base with the hand crank visible.
description_view_1: Viewed directly from the front, the horn dominates the scene, with the record and platter clearly visible, and the hand crank just peeking out on the left.
description_view_2: From the right side, the curved tonearm and the connection between the horn and the base are prominent, with the record and platter in clear view.
description_view_3: A top-down view shows the full circular opening of the horn, the record centered on the platter, and the square wooden base with metallic accents.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and black, supporting a vinyl record with a red label. It appears to be made of metal or hard plastic, with a smooth, slightly glossy finish and no visible grooves or patterns beyond the record itself. The platter is proportionally smaller than the base but central to the device.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, curved, and metallic, extending from the right side of the base toward the record. It is medium in length, with visible joints and a slightly ornate design, and appears to be resting near the edge of the record.
